claim: OpenClaw has critical security issues: (1) One-click RCE via WebSocket bypass lets attackers steal API keys and tokens in plaintext, (2) Malicious packages in Claw Hub steal credentials, (3) Large codebase + high agent power = security nightmare even with fixes.
actionable: If using OpenClaw: scope agent permissions strictly, avoid exposing sensitive APIs/keys, assume the codebase is a security risk. Better: build a minimal custom agent with your control.
source_title: I Built a Safer OpenClaw Alternative Using Claude Code

claim: Four core OpenClaw components that make it feel magical are: (1) Memory system (soul.md, user.md, memory.md, agents.md, session logs + SQLite RAG), (2) Heartbeat (scheduled proactive task execution), (3) Channel adapters (WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, Discord), (4) Skills registry.
actionable: Replicate OpenClaw's elegant pattern: use markdown files for memory + identity, schedule a heartbeat job (every 30 min) to proactively find tasks, support multi-platform adapters, and let skills extend capabilities.
source_title: I Built a Safer OpenClaw Alternative Using Claude Code

claim: You can replicate OpenClaw's best ideas in 2 days of Claude Code work: clone the repo, point Claude Code at how each component works, ask it to build your own adapted version. Coding agents excel on good examples.
actionable: To build a custom agent: clone a well-structured repo, use Claude Code to understand and adapt each module (memory, heartbeat, adapters, skills) to your tech stack in sequence.
source_title: I Built a Safer OpenClaw Alternative Using Claude Code

claim: A better-than-OpenClaw tech stack for a personal second brain: Obsidian (storage) + Markdown (memory files) + SQLite (local) + PostgreSQL (remote) + Claude Agent SDK (scheduled proactive jobs) + Claude Code (primary builder).
actionable: Stack your second brain: Obsidian for writing/syncing, markdown for memory, SQLite for local search, schedule Claude Agent SDK jobs every 30 min to check tasks/emails/calendar, use Claude Code to build features.
source_title: I Built a Safer OpenClaw Alternative Using Claude Code

claim: Using Claude Code with the Agent SDK locally is more cost-effective and terms-compliant than OpenClaw because you're not violating Anthropic's terms of service (which ban reselling subscriptions for non-personal use).
actionable: Build your agent using Claude Code + Agent SDK directly in your own environment, not by reselling OpenClaw. You stay compliant and keep costs within your subscription.
source_title: I Built a Safer OpenClaw Alternative Using Claude Code

claim: Skills in Claude Agent SDK/Code are just markdown files. Building custom capabilities is one-shot: 'Hey, here's how I want to generate PowerPoints. Build a skill for that.'
actionable: Extend your agent by writing a single markdown file per new capability (e.g., 'Generate slide decks from markdown'). Ask Claude Code to implement it.
source_title: I Built a Safer OpenClaw Alternative Using Claude Code
